title: Dissochaeta micrantha
taxonomy: Dissochaeta micrantha is classified in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the melastome family. This family of flowering plants is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Dissochaeta micrantha is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Dissochaeta. The genus Dissochaeta is part of the Melastomataceae family, which contains around 150 genera of plants. The classification of Dissochaeta micrantha reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Melastomataceae family.

intro: Dissochaeta micrantha is a shrub-like plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae, native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Indian Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine, to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dissochaeta micrantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Dissochaeta micrantha is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used as a component in various herbal blends, such as fever-reducing and anti-inflammatory formulations.
